classObjective: Write linear equations in slope-intercept form given a point and a slope and given two points.
workedExamples: 5-1, Example 1 — Write an Equation Given a Point and a Slope
5-1, Example 2 — Model Real-World Situations with Linear Equations
5-1, Example 3 — Write an Equation Given Two Points
warmUp: Write the equation of a line through (2, 5) with slope 3
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: point-slope form as bridge to slope-intercept; calculating b from a point; horizontal and vertical line cases
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing linear equations given point-slope combinations
independentPractice: Practice problems writing equations in slope-intercept form given various input conditions
assessment: Exit ticket: write the equation of the line through (-1, 4) and (3, -2)
capReflection: Why is point-slope form useful even when you ultimately want slope-intercept form?
